chapter_context.summary: Discusses walking in integrity, getting wisdom for success, parental grief from foolish sons, pitying the poor, and the fear of the Lord leading to satisfied sleep.

outline.text: Poor Walking in Integrity, False Witnesses, and Deceptive Friends
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Poor Walking in Integrity, False Witnesses, and Deceptive Friends (19:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Getting Wisdom, Discretion Deferring Anger, and Prudent Wife
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Getting Wisdom, Discretion Deferring Anger, and Prudent Wife (19:8-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Laziness, Keeping the Commandment, Pitying Poor, and Counsel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Laziness, Keeping the Commandment, Pitying Poor, and Counsel (19:15-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

outline.text: Fear of the LORD, Slothful Hand, and Scorners' Judgement
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Fear of the LORD, Slothful Hand, and Scorners' Judgement (19:23-29), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Proverbs.

key_themes.title: Wisdom vs. Folly
key_themes.description: Proverbs outlines a practical blueprint for daily living, contrasting the actions, words, and consequences of the wise with those of the foolish.

key_themes.title: The Fear of the Lord
key_themes.description: The foundational principle of wisdom is a reverent awe and trust in God, which guides moral choices and shapes character.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the practical sayings of Chapter 19, what is one specific area of your speech, relationships, or work where you can apply divine wisdom today?
How does this chapter prompt you to choose the path of integrity and diligence over convenience or compromise?

cultural_historical.title: Ancient Near Eastern Wisdom Literature
cultural_historical.text: Proverbs shares structural and thematic similarities with other ancient wisdom writings, but stands unique in placing the Fear of Yahweh as the foundation of all true wisdom.
